title: ericameria resinosa
intro: Ericameria resinosa, also known as Resin Bush or Rocky Mountain Golden Bush, is a shrub native to the mountainous regions of the western United States and Canada. This plant is valued for its resinous sap, which contains a compound called camphor, and has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. The medicinal properties of Ericameria resinosa have been used to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues, wounds, and skin infections, offering relief from congestion, pain, and inflammation. Herbal preparations of Ericameria resinosa can be found in the form of tinctures, salves, and essential oils, which can be used topically to promote healing and overall well-being.
